sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation found in sources

special_mechanics.name: Spinning Jump
special_mechanics.description: Player performs an aerial somersault firing both guns simultaneously. Both weapons at risk of being lost upon losing a life during this move.

special_mechanics.name: Overhead Perspective Stages
special_mechanics.description: Two stages presented from overhead perspective using SNES Mode 7 rendering. Player navigates to find and destroy predetermined targets with rotatable screen controls.

special_mechanics.name: Bomb Attack
special_mechanics.description: Limited-use bombs that damage all enemies on screen

tier_notes: T2 empirical/qualitative data. Sources document gameplay mechanics, level design philosophy, and development approach but contain no quantitative physics parameters. Game designed by Nobuya Nakazato to be tighter and more cinematic than predecessors with more comedic elements. Three regional versions exist (JP, US, EU) with mechanical differences (unlimited continues in JP version, final boss appears on normal difficulty in JP only).
